data.title: reAlpha announces 1-for-25 reverse split effective April 30
data.description:
• reAlpha Tech (AIRE) said on Tuesday it had implemented a 1-for-25 reverse stock split of its common shares, effective April 30, 2026.
  • Shareholders approved the reverse split in October 2025, aimed at raising the share price to meet Nasdaq’s $1 minimum bid requirement.
  • The reverse split will reduce outstanding shares to about 5.36M from roughly 134.12M.
  • Each 25 shares will be consolidated into one, with no change to shareholder rights, and no fractional shares issued.
  • Outstanding options, warrants, and other equity-linked securities will be adjusted proportionately for the reverse split.
  • Shares -18.65%.

data.title: Stock market mixed as OpenAI missing targets sparks tech selloff
data.description:
Wall Street opened mixed on Tuesday, with the Nasdaq and S&P 500 under pressure as a report on OpenAI missing its own targets has spooked investors. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.5%, the Dow (DJI) +0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -1.1%.

OpenAI (OPENAI) recently missed its own targets for new users and revenue, stumbles that have raised concern among some company leaders about whether it will be able to support its massive spending on data centers

On the earnings front, General Motors posted solid quarterly results and beat Wall Street’s profit expectations by nearly 30%. Coca-Cola (KO) topped estimates with its first-quarter earnings report.

Investors are also looking ahead to the Conference Board’s consumer confidence data for further clues on the economic outlook.

Geopolitical tensions remained in focus after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Russia, where he is expected to meet President Vladimir Putin, even as negotiations with the U.S. reached a stalemate over the weekend.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose to 3.83%, while the 10-year yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.37% and the 30-year yield (US30Y) advanced to 4.96%, reflecting upward pressure as investors assessed the macro backdrop.

data.title: Ares fund has ~$1B of software investments at 'medium' risk
data.description:
A review of Ares Capital's (ARCC) portfolio suggests that almost $1B of its investments tied to software companies are at least at “medium” risk of being upended by artificial intelligence in coming years, the publicly traded private credit fund disclosed in its Q1 earnings presentation on Tuesday.

Ares Capital (ARCC) stock fell 0.5% in Tuesday premarket trading.

The company, which is associated with asset manager Ares Management (ARES), said it hired an outside “top tier” consulting firm to assess the potential of AI disruption risks across all software-oriented companies in its investment portfolio. The disclosure coincides with an increase in unrealized net losses in Ares Capital's (ARCC) first-quarter earnings.

The business development company, which provides loans and direct investments to middle-market companies, said its total investments at fair value  stood at $29.5B on March 31, 2026, essentially unchanged from its value on Dec. 31, 2025.

About 22% of its portfolio is invested in software and services-related companies, its largest allocation. The second-biggest sector allocation, at 11%, is in health care equipment and services.

The review judged that 85% of the companies reviewed at fair value represented low risk from potential AI disruption. Those deemed to be at high risk were about 1% of the reviewed names by fair value, or ~0.3% of Ares Capital's (ARCC) total investment portfolio at fair value as of March 31, 2026. Investments considered to be at medium risk accounted for about 3% of ARCC's total investment portfolio, the company said.

That puts about $973M of the $29.5B portfolio at risk.

During Q1, ARCC's net unrealized losses increased to $412M, more than quadrupling the $83M of net unrealized losses in last year's Q1. 

The company, though, recorded Q1 net realized gains of $106M compared with realized losses of $44M  in the year-ago quarter.

data.title: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust trades higher post insider transactions disclosure
data.description:
KKR Real Estate Finance Trust (KREF) was trading higher after the mortgage REIT disclosed insider purchases on Monday.

Shares were 4.42% higher to $6.14 during pre-market trading on Tuesday.

CEO Matthew Salem acquired 60,000 shares of common stock for a weighted average price per share of $6.0412, bringing the total number of shares directly owned by the executive to 703,075.

COO Patrick Mattson bought 40,000 shares of common stock at  a weighted average price per share of $6.0268, and now owns 500,287 shares of direct ownership.

data.title: FOMC watch: Pantheon Macro sees the Fed standing pat as markets eye limited cuts
data.description:
The Federal Open Market Committee is widely expected to leave interest rates unchanged at its meeting this week, reinforcing a cautious stance as policymakers weigh mixed economic signals, according to a recent report from Pantheon Macroeconomics.

The investment firm noted that while financial markets continue to debate the timing of potential easing, the committee is unlikely to signal any urgency to shift policy. Instead, officials are expected to maintain a subtle easing bias, even as stronger labor market data reduces immediate pressure to act. 

Pantheon Macro went on to say the post-meeting statement is unlikely to frame risks as “two-sided,” suggesting the Fed remains more focused on downside economic vulnerabilities than inflation reacceleration. Voting is expected to show broad consensus, with nearly all members favoring no change. However, Stephen Miran is projected to again dissent in favor of a modest 25-basis-point rate cut the company added. 

During his press conference, Chair Jerome Powell is likely to reiterate that policy remains “modestly restrictive,” underscoring the central bank’s measured approach. Meanwhile, more hawkish policymakers are not expected to secure language indicating the next move could be a rate hike.

Market pricing currently reflects expectations for a prolonged pause, with a smaller probability assigned to limited easing later this year.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ). 

Fixed Income ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), (BIL), (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), (VTIP), (TIP), (SCHP), (STIP), (TIPX), (SPIP), (WIP), (GTIP), (LQDI), and (RINF).

data.title: OpenAI falls short on users and revenue, sparking internal worries: report
data.description:
• OpenAI (OPENAI) recently missed its own targets for new users and revenue, stumbles that have raised concern among some company leaders about whether it will be able to support its massive spending on data centers, The Wall Street Journal reported. 
  • CFO Sarah Friar has told other company leaders that she is worried the company might not be able to pay for future computing contracts if revenue doesn’t grow fast enough, according to people familiar with the matter. 
  • The company fell short of several monthly sales targets in 2026 after rival Anthropic PBC (ANTHRO) gained ground in the coding and enterprise markets, the report said.
  • However, OpenAI (OPENAI) CEO Sam Altman in a statement to CNBC, called the report "ridiculous"; said "we are totally aligned on buying as much compute as we can."
  • ChatGPT's growth slowed toward the end of last year, the WSJ report said, ​adding that OpenAI fell short of ​an ⁠internal target to reach 1 billion weekly active users for the artificial intelligence chatbot by year-end.

Updates story to add OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's statement to CNBC.

data.title: Amgen faces U.S. withdrawal of rare disorder therapy Tavneos
data.description:
The U.S. FDA has proposed to withdraw the marketing of Tavneos, a treatment developed by Amgen (AMGN) for a group of rare disorders called anti-neutrophil cytoplasmic autoantibody (ANCA)-associated vasculitis.

In a statement on Monday, the FDA’s Center for Drug Evaluation and Research ((CDER)) said that safety concerns associated with the treatment and potential manipulation of data supporting its marketing application led to the decision.

In a drug safety communication issued on March 31, CDER identified 76 cases of serious drug-induced liver injury, including vanishing bile duct syndrome ((VBDS)) associated with Tavneos, which was approved in 2021 for adults with severe active ANCA.

“Although hepatotoxicity is a serious adverse reaction for TAVNEOS identified in premarket clinical trials and described in product labeling, VBDS and DILI cases with fatal outcomes represent new safety concerns,” the CDER said.

Additionally, the regulator disclosed that new information obtained three years after the drug’s approval showed that people who conducted its registrational study manipulated the trial results to misrepresent the effectiveness of the therapy.

“The applicant also did not disclose the original analysis to the FDA, in violation of FDA regulations,” the regulator said, adding, “CDER can no longer conclude that there is, or has ever been, a valid demonstration that TAVNEOS is effective for its approved use.”

Tavneos, added to Amgen (AMGN) with its $3.7B acquisition of ChemoCentryx in 2022, generated $423M in sales from the U.S. last year with a 65% YoY growth.

The company can keep the drug on the market until it voluntarily pulls the product from the market, or it can request a hearing from the FDA regarding the proposed withdrawal.

data.title: UGI to sell Electric Division to Argo Infrastructure Partners in $470M deal
data.description:
UGI Corp. (UGI) said Tuesday it agreed to sell its Electric Division to funds managed by Argo Infrastructure Partners for $470M.

UGI's (UGI) Electric Division assets include ~2,700 miles of transmission and distribution lines and 14 substations in Pennsylvania's Luzerne and Wyoming counties that serve more than 63K customers.

Argo brings a proven track record in investing in and managing essential infrastructure assets, which include five regulated energy utilities serving more than 700K U.S. customers, two electric transmission systems, and an energy storage network.

"This transaction, which has an attractive valuation, reflects our focus on areas where UGI has the greatest competitive advantage," President and CEO Robert Flexon said.

data.title: Spotify slides as weak subscriber outlook upstages Q1 results
data.description:
Spotify (SPOT) shares are under pressure in premarket trading as the company’s disappointing outlook for premium subscriber growth for the second quarter overshadowed better-than-expected first quarter results.

For Q1, Spotify (SPOT) met or beat subscriber metrics, including total monthly active users ((MAUs)) and premium subscribers, but missed slightly on the number of ad-supported MAUs. 

These results led to an increase in the company’s total revenue (+8%) while profits nearly tripled to a better-than-expected €3.45, beating estimates by €0.50, all of which reflected a price hike to its premium service in January. Gross margin expanded by 133 basis points year-over-year and exceeded both the company’s guidance and analysts’ estimates by 20 basis points.

On the balance sheet, free cash flow reached a record €824M in Q1, bringing FCF for the last twelve months to €3.2B.

Looking ahead to the current quarter, Spotify (SPOT) expects premium subscribers to increase to 299M, falling short of 300.3M estimates. This overshadowed the company’s other estimates that exceeded expectations, including total revenue, gross margin, and total MAUs.

data.title: Home price growth cools more than expected in March : S&P Cotality Case-Shiller Home Price
data.description:
February S&P Cotality Case-Shiller Home Price Index for 20 cities slipped -0.1% M/M vs. +0.2% prior, according to data released on Tuesday.

On an unadjusted basis, the 20-city index rose +0.4% M/M vs. -0.1% prior. That resulted in a +0.9% Y/Y increase vs. 1.1% consensus and +1.2% in January.

Developing… Check back for updates.
